mission.title: Install and Configure Kagent on Kubernetes
mission.description: kagent is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents, allowing you to easily deploy and manage AI workloads on Kubernetes. Installing kagent enables you to leverage its capabilities for developing intelligent applications in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Add the kagent Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official kagent Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kagent https://kagent-dev.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install kagent using Helm
steps.description:
Install kagent in a new namespace called 'kagent' with the following command:
```bash
helm install kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --create-namespace --version v0.7.19
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the kagent pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kagent
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the kagent deployment to set resource limits. Create a YAML file named `kagent-resources.yaml` with the following content:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kagent
  namespace: kagent
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: kagent
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
``` 
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kagent-resources.yaml
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the kagent service, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kagent --namespace kagent 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the kagent pods running in the 'kagent' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080. You should also see the resource limits applied to the kagent deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kagent https://kagent-dev.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --create-namespace --version v0.7.19

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kagent
  namespace: kagent
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: kagent
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the kagent release from the cluster using Helm. This command will remove all associated resources created by the release.
```bash
helm uninstall kagent --namespace kagent
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace kagent
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the kagent namespace to remove any remaining resources and verify that it has been successfully deleted.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kagent
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the kagent deployment. You can use the following command to export the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get deployment kagent --namespace kagent -o yaml > kagent-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the kagent release to the latest version. Use the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --version v0.8.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the kagent pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue may occur if there is a misconfiguration in the deployment. To diagnose, check the logs of the pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagent
``` 
If the logs indicate a configuration issue, review your deployment settings and correct any errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the kagent service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running and check the port forwarding setup:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kagent
kubectl port-forward svc/kagent --namespace kagent 8080:80
``` 
Make sure the correct ports are being forwarded.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to insufficient resources, check the resource limits set in your deployment:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment kagent --namespace kagent
``` 
Consider increasing the resource limits in your `kagent-resources.yaml` file and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error indicating that the Helm release cannot be found, verify that the release exists in the specified namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace kagent
``` 
If the release does not appear, ensure that you are using the correct namespace and release name.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
